pi_items.source: Task description (line 5): 'Create a Program named 'Nutrition Support''
Task description (line 5) explicitly states: 'Set Description: 'Outpatient nutrition rehabilitation and monitoring''. The metadata value is incomplete/truncated.
Task description (line 5): 'Create a new Concept named 'Nutrition Support Program''

Task description (line 5): 'Set Class: 'Program' (or 'Misc' if Program is unavailable)'. Confirmed as valid OpenMRS concept class via https://guide.openmrs.org/configuration/managing-concepts-and-metadata/

Task description (line 5): 'Datatype: 'N/A''. Confirmed as valid OpenMRS datatype via ConceptDatatype API documentation (https://resources.openmrs.org/doc/org/openmrs/ConceptDatatype.html). N/A datatype is used for concepts that are only answers, not questions.

privileged_info_summary: This is a synthetic benchmark task for an AI agent. The task creates a test medical program in a Bahmni/OpenMRS system. Verified facts: The program name is 'Nutrition Support', the concept name is 'Nutrition Support Program', the concept class is 'Program' (a valid OpenMRS class), and the datatype is 'N/A' (a valid OpenMRS datatype for answer-only concepts). DISCREPANCY FOUND: The metadata specifies program description as 'nutrition rehabilitation', but the task description explicitly instructs to set the description to the full phrase 'Outpatient nutrition rehabilitation and monitoring'. The 'Nutrition Support Program' concept is synthetic, created specifically for this benchmark task and not derived from real medical data.
